focusPoints.label: Late nights — push, overload, or default?
focusPoints.reason: Notes say Lin worked late for about three weeks; one possibility is a temporary push to land launches, but it could be becoming a default pace that needs a reset.

focusPoints.label: What’s driving the load now that launches are done.
focusPoints.reason: Because Lin tends to brush off concern, it’s worth clarifying what work is actually consuming time right now versus what should be deprioritized now that the major features are shipped.

focusPoints.label: What Lin should keep versus delegate.
focusPoints.reason: Whether they can sustain output without personal heroics depends on delegation—at Head of Product level, you want real ownership distributed so late nights aren’t masking an autonomy or distribution gap.

focusPoints.label: What Lin wants more of from you.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the next two weeks feel manageable often comes down to what they need from you—decisions, air cover, or removing cross-functional friction—so it’s a good bi-weekly anchor to surface requests early.

name: Lin
notes: Lin has delivered two major features ahead of schedule but has worked late for about three weeks. I need to understand whether this pace was a temporary push or is becoming a default. Because Lin tends to brush off concern, I should ask directly what is driving the load now that those launches are done. I also want clarity on what work only Lin should own versus what could be delegated, since sustained late nights at this level can hide a distribution or autonomy problem.
